Palmer Amaranth.description: Highly competitive pigweed species with rapid growth and prolific seed production. Known for glyphosate resistance in many regions. Can reduce crop yields by over 70% if left uncontrolled.
Palmer Amaranth.resistance: Widespread glyphosate resistance; confirmed resistance to ALS inhibitors and PPO inhibitors in multiple states.
Palmer Amaranth.timing_notes: Most effective control at seedling stage (≤4 inches). Late-season control is difficult once plants exceed 6 inches.
Palmer Amaranth.safety: Wear long sleeves, chemical-resistant gloves, and eye protection. Observe restricted entry intervals on product labels.

herbicides.name: Roundup PowerMax
herbicides.resistance: High resistance risk — verify susceptibility before use

Waterhemp.description: Dioecious pigweed species with exceptional genetic diversity and herbicide resistance. Emerges throughout the growing season, making timing critical.
Waterhemp.resistance: Resistance documented to glyphosate, ALS inhibitors, PPO inhibitors, and HPPD inhibitors.
Waterhemp.timing_notes: Apply when plants are ≤3 inches tall. Multiple emergence flushes require residual programs.
Waterhemp.safety: Use PPE per label. Avoid drift to sensitive crops and habitats.

herbicides.name: Dual II Magnum
herbicides.notes: Best as part of layered residual program

herbicides.name: Flexstar GT
herbicides.notes: Scout for PPO resistance before application

herbicides.name: Callisto
herbicides.resistance: HPPD-resistant waterhemp confirmed in some areas
herbicides.notes: Use with atrazine for improved control

Giant Ragweed.description: Large-seeded broadleaf weed that can exceed 15 feet in height. Highly competitive in corn and soybean, especially in no-till systems.
Giant Ragweed.resistance: Glyphosate-resistant giant ragweed confirmed in multiple Midwest states.
Giant Ragweed.timing_notes: Control at ≤6 inches for best results. Early spring emergence.
Giant Ragweed.safety: Standard herbicide PPE required. Giant ragweed pollen is a major allergen.

herbicides.name: 2,4-D Amine
herbicides.resistance: Generally effective; ALS-resistant biotypes may need alternative modes

Johnsongrass.description: Aggressive perennial grass spreading by rhizomes and seed. Difficult to control once established; regrows from rhizome fragments.
Johnsongrass.resistance: Glyphosate-resistant johnsongrass documented in Arkansas and Mississippi.
Johnsongrass.timing_notes: Target at 12–18 inches when actively growing. Fall rhizome translocation improves control.
Johnsongrass.safety: Use respiratory protection when mixing. Avoid drift to non-target grasses.

herbicides.name: Roundup PowerMax
herbicides.notes: Use in Roundup Ready crops only

herbicides.name: Select Max
herbicides.notes: Add crop oil concentrate per label

herbicides.name: Accent Q
herbicides.notes: Requires corn ≥12 inches with good crop safety

Horseweed.description: Also called marestail. Winter or summer annual that can reach 6+ feet. Glyphosate-resistant biotypes dominate many regions.
Horseweed.resistance: Widespread glyphosate resistance; ALS and PPO resistance also documented.
Horseweed.timing_notes: Fall or early spring burndown critical. Rosette stage (≤4 inches) is ideal.
Horseweed.safety: Avoid inhalation of spray mist. Follow restricted entry intervals.

herbicides.name: Sharpen
herbicides.notes: Tank-mix with 2,4-D or glyphosate for burndown

herbicides.name: Engenia
herbicides.resistance: Some ALS-resistant populations may need tank-mix
herbicides.notes: Observe planting intervals and wind restrictions

Giant Foxtail.description: Tufted annual grass common in corn and soybean fields. Emerges early and competes aggressively for nutrients and light.
Giant Foxtail.resistance: Limited herbicide resistance reported compared to pigweeds.
Giant Foxtail.timing_notes: Control at ≤4 inches. Pre-emergence programs provide best consistency.

Common Lambsquarters.description: Fast-growing broadleaf weed with powdery-coated leaves. Produces large seed banks and emerges early in spring.
Common Lambsquarters.resistance: ALS-resistant lambsquarters confirmed in some regions.
Common Lambsquarters.timing_notes: Most susceptible at seedling stage. Late applications less effective on mature plants.
Common Lambsquarters.safety: Wear gloves and eye protection when handling herbicides.

herbicides.name: Atrazine 4L
herbicides.notes: Foundation herbicide in corn weed control

herbicides.name: Roundup PowerMax
herbicides.notes: Effective burndown option in RR crops

Kochia.description: Drought-tolerant tumbleweed that breaks off and spreads seeds. Glyphosate and ALS resistance widespread in western US.
Kochia.resistance: Glyphosate, ALS, and dicamba resistance documented in Great Plains.
Kochia.timing_notes: Control at ≤2 inches. Early spring application before tumble stage.
Kochia.safety: Avoid drift; kochia pollen can cause allergic reactions.

herbicides.name: Sharpen
herbicides.resistance: PPO mode effective on resistant biotypes

herbicides.name: Starane Flex
herbicides.resistance: Synthetic auxin mode still effective in most areas
